evals.name: tier1-hyperfine
evals.prompt: I do a lot of shell benchmarking and I keep reaching for hyperfine but it's never installed on my boxes. Can you set it up properly in my dotfiles so every machine I provision gets it from now on? Upstream is github.com/sharkdp/hyperfine.
evals.expected_output: Adds hyperfine as a TIER-1 release binary: new entry in home/.chezmoidata.yaml (repo + pinned version), a download entry in home/.chezmoiexternals/shared.yaml.tmpl (.local/bin/hyperfine, archive-file), and an archiveNameHyperfine line in home/.chezmoi.yaml.tmpl. On a branch. Does NOT apt-install / ad-hoc install, does NOT use a vendor script when a release binary exists.

evals.name: runtime-node
evals.prompt: I need Node.js 26 on this box to run a build step. Get it set up — and ideally in a way that sticks so I don't have to redo it on every machine.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes Node is a language runtime → uses mise (e.g. `mise use -g node@26`), which the repo does NOT provision. Does NOT add node to .chezmoidata.yaml / shared.yaml.tmpl / any tier, does NOT write a vendor installer or apt-install nodejs.
